_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of assessment techniques (rubrics, evaluation plans, tests, quizzes).
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of model evaluation frameworks, including train/validation/test split design, cross-validation, evaluation metric selection, and the conditions under which each metric is appropriate.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI RM domain. RM-K-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-K-002 (AI, cosine: 0.483), OP-K-004 (OP, cosine: 0.467).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.9/10 (raw 0.586). Shared vocabulary: [evaluation, knowledge]. DCWF-only terms [assessment, plans, quizzes, rubrics, techniques, tests] vs WIDAI-only terms [appropriate, conditions, cross, design, frameworks, metric] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.874 (P=0.892, R=0.857). P exceeds R by 0.035 — the DCWF element's 9 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 22 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Knowledge of assessment techniques (rubrics, evaluation plans, tests, quizzes)." and "Knowledge of model evaluation frameworks, including train/validation/test split" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.491 < STS 0.586 (gap -0.095). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [assessment, plans, quizzes] and [appropriate, conditions, cross] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 10.7%. Shared tokens: [evaluation, knowledge]. DCWF-only: [assessment, plans, quizzes, rubrics, techniques, tests]. WIDAI-only: [appropriate, conditions, cross, design, frameworks, metric].
